interface: Vault HTTP API v1 — LIST operations, KV v2 metadata listing, and Enterprise sys/namespaces (stable)

request_headers.X-Vault-Token: the client token, on every request; never placed in the URL or query string
request_headers.X-Vault-Namespace: absolute namespace path such as eng/team-a/ — sent only when targeting a non-root namespace; a root-namespace request omits the header entirely

list_operation.fallback: GET /v1/{path}?list=true for HTTP stacks or proxies that cannot send a LIST verb; both forms are equivalent

success.folder_rule: keys ending in / are folders that can be listed one level deeper; keys without a trailing slash are leaf entries. The same name can appear both ways.

empty.rule: a 404 whose errors array is EMPTY is Vault's empty-list answer (nothing here, or nothing you may see) and must be treated as an empty result, not an exception

errors.rule: any non-2xx response with a NON-empty errors array (403 permission denied, 404 with a route error, ...) is a real failure: propagate a structured exception carrying the HTTP status code and every message

rules: the KV v2 secret tree is enumerated by LISTing the mount's metadata paths; folder keys are appended without their trailing slash
an empty-list 404 for a folder mid-walk means no children; continue the walk
resulting secret paths are relative to the mount and ordinally sorted

namespaces.list_endpoint: LIST /v1/sys/namespaces (Vault Enterprise; the same GET ?list=true fallback applies)
rules: sys/namespaces lists only the direct children of the request's namespace; key_info paths are RELATIVE to it
to target a child, append its relative path to the current namespace header (eng/ + team-a/ -> header eng/team-a/); nested children recurse the same way
an empty-list 404 means the namespace has no children (also the shape a namespace-less response takes when nothing is visible)
namespace info entries are ordered ordinally by path

inventory.entry: (namespace, secret path) pairs; the namespace of a root-client entry is the empty string
inventory.ordering: ordinal by namespace, then ordinal by secret path
